name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: The number of pro-choice Democratic women 📈
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60dc/24c69fd9/9bb8a82a/189a41d1/532705263/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact&source=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact")
---

Peter,

EMILY's List works. The proof is in the numbers.

We were founded in 1985, and just one year later, the number of Democratic women in Congress started to climb.

The number of Republican women? Not so much. That's because there's no Republican equivalent to EMILY's List.

Our record of winning is long, but we're not done yet; not while women still make up less than half of Congress.
**[Donate $3 to build on our record of success and help elect even more pro-choice Democratic women.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60dc/24c69fd9/9bb8a82a/189a41d3/532705263/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170919_email_NYTchart?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Donate+3+to+build+on+our+record+of+succe&utm_campaign=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact&source=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact")**
This chart from the _New York Times_ makes our impact crystal clear:
[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60dc/24c69fd9/9bb8a82a/189a41cc/532705263/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170919_email_NYTchart?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact&source=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact")
It is clear that, without us, the number of Democratic women in Congress would not have risen as fast as it has.

That chart is proof that when we organize and rally behind candidates together, we can make a difference. And as the new executive director of EMILY's List, I am committed to keep pushing that blue line upward with you.
**Help elect even more pro-choice Democratic women this year and beyond. Donate $3:**
**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Elect-Even-More-Women](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60dc/24c69fd9/9bb8a82a/189a41ce/532705263/VEsA/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170919_email_NYTchart?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=6+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgElectEvenMoreWom&utm_campaign=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact&source=WQEFE935N_digital_email_20170919_NYTchart_inact")**
Thanks, and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

name: The White House
subject: Outlining an America First Foreign Policy
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e063d8d578660416c537d24639798aa241f12a09abe9ff86194e7a5d60f7d51c2f2ad483c0a3c4345357dc2e21aade9139d

On Tuesday, President Donald J. Trump addressed the entire body of foreign dignitaries at the 72nd Session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York City. He urged leaders to invest in the strength of their own countries and pursue policies that increase stability and prosperity for their citizens, but to unite in opposition to common threats and “work together and confront together those who threaten us with chaos, turmoil, and terror.”
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e06fdba591214f80f0b57b64ffe76fb946c4541e5bc4368b8bd69c693a7237e9ec4134887e9c2e856b650d14f8a08d4d88d
Read the full speech.

-------------

Hurricane Maria

The White House is coordinating with FEMA and other agencies to monitor and provide support for those affected by Hurricane Maria. Earlier this week, the President approved an emergency declaration for Puerto Rico and ordered Federal assistance to supplement commonwealth and local response efforts due to the emergency conditions resulting from Hurricane Maria.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e067059b55552f01c0bfe754c3ae10606287af18459898279e041d42bf0a8264b5df2d0e2d544ded61cac567f51fd37a528
Read the emergency declaration

President Trump's First Speech to the U.N.

In President Trump's first speech to the United Nations General Assembly he called for allies across the world to work together to meet the many challenges we face.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e067b6cfcf30e52d122ad694b0a07b31d7941dd12cd23296b6af13f1302a9861e085f0527b895df17195fe06d5e2f6fe629
Watch the video

The United Nations General Assembly in Photos

President Trump is leading the United States' delegation to the United Nations General Assembly and will continue meeting with world leaders today and tomorrow.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e067e3562681cc8520636eafc20ff92d439923a4ca95991ab81277e6bf55c871a4903a276543bfecbad360e425e6dcc7984
View the photo gallery

Outlining an America First Foreign Policy

President Trump outlined the Administration’s foreign policy of “Principled Realism,” a strategy of acting in America’s national interest and in accordance with our values. The President emphasized that America First does not mean America Alone saying, “Our foreign policy calls for a direct, robust, and meaningful engagement with the world. It is American leadership based on vital security interests that we share with our allies all across the globe.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e06eab9a90d88e4e300e7974a98f6edfe87eb7bc2ce90ee2016affd8e9925473dc9d660b8eaa9376dc83a42ebed3775e4a3
Read more

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump attend the 72nd Session of the United Nations General Assembly | September 19, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS and VP TODAY

President Trump remains in New York for the third day of the United Nations General Assembly. This morning, the President joins leaders from Jordan and the Palestinian Authority for expanded bilateral meetings. The President will then participate in a working lunch with African leaders. First Lady Melania Trump will also host a luncheon in the early afternoon. President Trump will spend the afternoon participating in additional expanded bilateral meetings with leaders from the United Kingdom and Egypt.

Vice President Mike Pence, also in New York for UNGA, will participate in the Security Council Meeting on Peacekeeping Reform this morning.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e0636b4e5cf3f57584adb46d7153bc2635afbf7e40ac8821361c3b1aecfd66f0853faeee6d3ed080ceb90630b69f8f5a51a
Watch the Vice President's speech. He will join the President for bilateral meetings in the afternoon.

COMING UP

On Thursday, President Trump will participate in more bilateral meetings with world leaders.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e06f45a8c8fd8777da2ed857eb59cda412e02653d0c5de377a5a56ebac305167f8dd817e4d2008e9f57f88231f4675f884e
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c36aa08bd36b7e060d5c7aad206ba9dd37732f987caed1eca6be9653e10f8a2eec00aa02f648890bb73a3d9f97ec29f7a870ba41e41a08cf
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d402b787f430c662d7ca9f4d37b622ad929087c8cee6c1abc9c7dece559bd1742b5cae2e6814b3be5f886949adb00deafd
Unsubscribe

name: =?utf-8?Q?Team=20Scott?=
subject: Can I Count On You?
body:
| | |
---
[View this email in your browser](http://mailchi.mp/philscott/can-i-count-on-you?e=19cebfe173)
---
| |
---
| Greetings,
---
| First and foremost, I’d like to thank you for your help and generosity throughout the last campaign. It’s because of your support that I’m writing to you now as Vermont’s 82nd Governor. It’s a role I’m proud and honored to serve in, and one that gives all of us tremendous opportunities to make Vermont stronger.

While I’ve only been in office eight months, many of those opportunities are starting to come to fruition.

As promised throughout the campaign, my administration is concentrated on growing the economy, making Vermont more affordable, and protecting the most vulnerable. Those three principles guide us in everything we do. **_To make good on that promise, I’m proud to have presented, fought for and signed a historic budget that does not raise a single tax or fee. This is the first time this has been done that we can find on record!_**

**But it didn’t come easy. Initially, the majority in the legislature passed a budget that would have raised property taxes AND ignored my proposal to save millions of dollars by changing the way teachers’ health care benefits are handled.**

**_I vetoed that budget bill because I knew we could do better for all Vermonters. And we did!_**

**The final budget I signed on June 28 ensures property tax rates do not increase for the first time in recent memory, saves $13 million over the next two years by restructuring teachers’ health care benefit plans, and provides a path to achieve greater efficiency, and more savings, in the future.**

We are working to restore fiscal responsibility in Montpelier, and helping families get ahead, by insisting state spending doesn’t grow faster than the economy or wages. And I will continue to fight to make Vermont more affordable and run a state government which lives within its means.

In addition to a historic budget that doesn’t raise taxes or fees, we are also prioritizing investments in pro-growth areas like job training and secured a $35 million dollar housing package that will employ hundreds of workers and generate $50 million in wages in the construction of more than 500 housing units -- and help make homes more affordable for middle income households.

My budget also invested in early care and learning, and higher education -- an important step toward a cradle-to-career continuum of learning that will attract more jobs and more working families who want to raise their children in the nation’s safest and healthiest state.

**We are also changing the culture in State Government by listening to those on the front lines, and we’re making some exciting headway in terms of innovating state government to make it more efficient, productive and valuable to you.** During my first week in office, I issued three Executive Orders. The first created an Agency of Digital Services, a single entity to better manage state IT projects. The second combined the Lottery Commission and the Department of Liquor Control (the legislature slowed this merger, but I’m confident we’ll get this in 2018). The third order would have unified the Agency of Commerce and Community Development with the Department of Labor -- literally putting the people who help create jobs next to the people who help Vermonters find jobs. Unfortunately, this too was blocked by the legislature, but it’s an idea that continues to be discussed in the State House.

Over the next several months, I will remain laser-focused on making state government work better for you. Every Tuesdaymorning when I convene my cabinet, I always remind them we’re public servants, we’re here to serve and follow through on our commitment to **grow the economy, make Vermont more affordable** and **protect the most vulnerable**. And we’ll continue to think this way, because it’s what Vermonters deserve and expect from their state government.

**I’m proud of what we’ve been able to accomplish just 8 months into the job, but there is much more work to do. **Everything I’ve proposed cannot happen overnight, which is why I need your help to ensure I have another term to get this work done.

[**Can I count on you to donate $100, $250, $500, $1000 or any amount you are comfortable with up to $4,000?**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9df72a0e26&e=19cebfe173)

I can’t thank you enough for the support and encouragement you’ve shown me over the years. Please don’t hesitate to reach out; I’m always willing to listen.

Sincerely,

Phil Scott
Governor
---

| | Peter,

Your right to vote is fundamental to being an American. In fact, our country was founded on the principle of democratic representation. But there are dark money groups who are focused on making it harder for our voices to be heard, and to re-elect lawmakers like Joe who stand up for working families in Washington.

Americans' voting rights have been targeted for years -- and now it's getting even worse. Over the past decade, we've seen everything from cutting the number of early voting centers to adopting strict voter ID laws. **Now, partisan voting commissions are trying to dismiss the voices of millions of Americans and claim that their votes shouldn't have counted.**

**[Sign the petition today to demand that we end this war on voters' rights.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2478/7377f838/3417054a/51fbe80c/909499165/VEsH/ "http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/s/sign-and-defend-your-voting-rights-")**

Peter, we can't let the GOP get away with these attacks. In 2014, Indiana had the lowest voter turnout in the country -- and now the GOP is trying to make it _even harder_ for Hoosiers to go to the polls because _they 're scared_ of losing.

Hello --
It looks like you haven't had a chance to sign our emergency petition telling Senate Republicans NOT to pass Graham-Cassidy and NOT to repeal Obamacare.
No problem, but we can't afford for you to wait much longer. Here's the link to sign: [**http://act.maggiehassan.com/protect-our-care**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde1b/2a380d31/1c2bbff5/fe714b/3072181491/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170918_GrahamCassidy?source=e20170920_gc3_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")

♦ An estimated 32 MILLION people would lose coverage within 10 years
♦ It would END Medicaid expansion
♦ It rolls back coverage for women's health and family planning
♦ It ends federal protections for people with pre-existing conditions, lifetime caps, and essential benefits
There are real concerns that John McCain, the Senator often credited with blocking Mitch McConnell's last attempts to repeal Obamacare, might actually support this version.
A vote in the Senate could come as soon as next Wednesday, so the only way to protect access to health care for our families, neighbors, and friends is to take action now.

subject: This is not a drill (Obamacare repeal)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
NBC reported yesterday, "Obamacare is suddenly in grave danger." We need your help to stop this latest repeal bill NOW.
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/obamacare-repeal-deadline?refcode=DPE-20170920-Rapid-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
_**NBC: "Obamacare Is Suddenly in Grave Danger"**_
Team,

This is not a drill. A GOP Senator confirmed to _Axios_ that Republicans will "likely" vote on Obamacare repeal **one week from today** \-- and it's beginning to look like their final Hail Mary pass might actually succeed.

_NBC_ reported yesterday, "Obamacare is suddenly in grave danger," noting that John McCain -- one of the three previous Republican no votes -- is open to supporting the new GOP bill.

[**We MUST ramp up our efforts to save Obamacare once more. Pitch in $1 right now to show Republicans the consequences they 'll face for ripping away health care coverage.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/obamacare-repeal-deadline?refcode=DPE-20170920-Rapid-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

We know we've said before that Obamacare is on the line, but with just 10 days to go -- and only SEVEN days til the likely vote -- this is no time to get tired or complacent.

Republicans are hoping we'll let our attention slip so they can ram this bill through with 50 votes before the September 30 deadline. The only way for us to stop them is to prove we're still watching.

name: Jenna Kruse, EMILY's List
subject: Don't let the GOP rewrite the Constitution
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60c6/24c69fd9/9ae2ba0e/189a6e1f/4183200733/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact&source=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact")
---

Peter,

Republicans are up to no good. And this time it has been about 10 years in the making.

Thanks to major investments from billionaire special interests, Republicans now completely control 32 state legislatures -- that's just two short of what they need to call a constitutional convention.

If successful, Republicans could begin the process to effectively rewrite the Constitution **without a single Democratic vote.**

That's why we're targeting entire state legislatures next year. So far, we have prioritized flipping 12 chambers from red to blue.
**[Donate $3 to help turn state legislatures from red to blue, and stop Republican efforts to call a constitutional convention.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60c6/24c69fd9/9ae2ba0e/189a6e19/4183200733/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170920_email_GOPConstitutionalConvention?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Donate+3+to+help+turn+state+legislatures&utm_campaign=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact&source=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact")**
Republicans are getting closer to making this nightmare a reality with every election.

Twenty-eight states have already passed resolutions calling for a constitutional convention. And TWO of the GOP presidential candidates last year openly supported the idea.

We've seen the lengths to which Republicans are willing to go to attack our rights and control statehouses. In Nevada, Republicans have resorted to shady recall tactics to flip the Nevada Senate and are targeting two of our women in the process.

If Republicans think we're going to sit back and let them get away with stealing progress, they've got another thing coming.
**Donate $3 to help replace Republicans with pro-choice Democratic women and help recruit even more who can flip state legislatures from red to blue:**
**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Flip-State-Legislatures-Blue](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c60c6/24c69fd9/9ae2ba0e/189a6e1b/4183200733/VEsD/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170920_email_GOPConstitutionalConvention?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=5+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgFlipStateLegisla&utm_campaign=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact&source=WQEFE938N_digital_email_20170920_GOPConstitutionalConvention_inact")**
Thanks,

Jenna Kruse
Vice President of Research, EMILY's List
